items.title: Human Resource Development
items.description: Continuous training through teaching undergraduate and graduate courses, supervision of theses, and mentoring of research fellows.

items.title: Basic and Applied Research
items.description: Rigorous study of the foundations and theoretical models underlying quantum information and computation, as well as their technological applications.

items.title: Academic and Scientific Events
items.description: Organization of seminars, workshops, and conferences to disseminate advances and foster interdisciplinary exchange.

items.title: Technology Transfer
items.description: Exploration of pathways for transferring knowledge and quantum technologies to industry and society.

items.title: Characterization and Benchmarking of Quantum Systems
items.description: We develop tools for the characterization of quantum systems in the NISQ era, where direct observation is fundamentally limited. These methods are crucial for progressing towards fault-tolerant quantum computation.

items.title: Quantum Correlations and Applications
items.description: We study quantum correlations and their role as a resource, enabling applications such as secure key distribution and certified randomness generation.

items.title: Quantum Resource Theories
items.description: We explore the role of quantum resources — from coherence to entanglement and beyond — in information processing under restricted sets of operations.

items.title: Certification of Quantum Teleportation
items.description: We design certification methods that go beyond average fidelity, incorporating robustness to noise and ensuring the integrity of transmitted quantum states.

items.title: Quantum Algorithms for Knowledge Graphs
items.description: We investigate variational quantum algorithms applied to knowledge graphs, combining trainable quantum circuits with classical optimization for link prediction and community detection.

items.title: Quantum Optimization
items.description: We develop and test quantum algorithms aimed at solving high-complexity optimization problems, using both real quantum devices and simulators.

items.title: Quantum Models of Computation and Randomness
items.description: Using tools from computability and complexity theory, we study whether quantum computational models can detect more structure than classical ones, potentially enabling efficient generation of high-quality deterministic randomness.
